key_insight: A multi-scale ML framework can jointly model chemical, spin, and structural disorder in alloys to predict phase behavior across temperature.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable design and screening of complex magnetic and high-entropy alloys where multiple coupled disorders control performance.
method: Combines graph neural networks and machine-learning interatomic potentials.
Uses Monte Carlo and molecular dynamics to sample configurational and structural disorder.
Applies the workflow to bcc Fe-Co and Fe-Co-C as a prototype multi-disorder alloy system.
evidence_signal: The framework reproduces key experimental temperatures for Fe-Co, predicting the order-disorder transition at 1,000 K and melting at 1,690 K versus measured values of 1,006 K and about 1,700 K.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate training data and efficient coupling of ML models with sampling, which may be challenging for alloys with even more complex disorder landscapes.
paper_title: A Multi-Scale Machine Learning Framework for Coupled Chemical, Spin, and Structural Disorder in Alloys
